name: Mercury Banking API
what_it_does: Business banking API for startups enabling programmatic money movement, account management, and treasury operations via REST.
use_cases: Automating vendor payments and payroll disbursements from agent workflows
Querying account balances and transaction history for finance automation
Programmatic wire and ACH initiation for startup treasury operations
Building internal finance dashboards with live Mercury account data
not_for: High-volume payment processing requiring enterprise SLAs
Companies needing dedicated banking infrastructure (use Increase or Modern Treasury instead)
best_when: You are a startup already banking with Mercury and want to automate treasury operations or build internal finance tooling against your existing accounts.
avoid_when: You need a dedicated payment operations platform, high-volume throughput guarantees, or custom bank integrations — Mercury API is an extension of the consumer banking product, not a payment infrastructure platform.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token. Keys generated in the Mercury developer portal. No fine-grained scopes — key has full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Banking is free with no monthly fees; API access is included with Mercury accounts at no additional cost.
pricing.paid_tiers: No separate paid API tier. Mercury earns on interchange and yield on deposits.
pricing.notes: API access requires applying through the Mercury developer portal and joining a waitlist. Approval is not guaranteed and Mercury may restrict API access to accounts in good standing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits not publicly documented; observed limits are conservative compared to dedicated payment APIs. Contact Mercury support for production rate limit details.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are functional but less structured than Stripe or Increase. HTTP status codes are used correctly; error bodies lack machine-readable codes for all failure types.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported on payment initiation endpoints but not consistently across all mutating operations. Documentation is sparse on retry behavior.
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires waitlist approval — cannot self-serve to production; plan for approval lag in agent deployment.
No official SDK means agents must handle raw HTTP with manual retry and error logic.
Rate limits are undocumented publicly; agents hitting limits will receive 429s without clear backoff guidance.
Payment status is asynchronous — agents must implement webhook handling or polling to confirm completion.
Mercury is a banking product, not a payment infrastructure company; API is less mature and may have more breaking changes than dedicated infrastructure APIs.

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II certified. TLS required. No scope granularity — API key grants full account access. Key rotation process is manual through the dashboard.
